1. there is no base agent key! all keys are created at session start -- if it serves recovery we can derive the nsec by mgmt_private_key+session_id so we don't need to store any nsec other thant the mgmt_private key and we can always derive what's the nsec just by resuming a session (i.e. `tenex-edge launch --session <session-id>` or `claude --resume <session-id>` would have everything we need (i.e. "session_id") to recover the nsec. Although we need some kind of way for a backend to know "ah yes, I recognize this pubkey, I need to resume a session or I need to route to some existing pty session" so we do need to store all pubkeys ever generated at the local backend. 2. pubkey trust is determined EXCLUSIVELY AND ALWAYS by nip29 channel membership -- mgmt backend key adds the new session pubkey as a member to the channel they need -- and removes them when the session "expires" (i.e. session closed by the user or by the agent running that session) 3. per-session kind:0 is OBVIOUSLY the only right answer -- it can't be something else. Again, there is no base agent pubkey! 4. the kind:0 should be "@session-codename@hostname" so an agent using `tenex-edget chat write "@session-codename@hostname are you done?" would have the p-tagging it needs. any other questions?
